fml.menu.mods.info.description.justenoug:
Overview: Adds a JEI profession category that connects villager professions with their valid job-site blocks.

Operation and result: Open the profession category in JEI, or inspect the uses of a job-site block, to view the matching villager profession and workstation choices before building a trading hall.

Example: A lectern points to the Librarian profession, while a composter points to the Farmer profession.
fml.menu.mods.info.description.catalogue:
Overview: Replaces the basic NeoForge mod list with a searchable catalogue for installed mods.

Operation and result: Search or scroll through the list, select a mod, then read its description and version information. The Config, Website, and Report Issue buttons open the available settings and support links for that entry.

Use it when you need to identify an unfamiliar mod, find its configuration screen, or report a problem without searching through the mods folder.
fml.menu.mods.info.description.jeed:
Overview: Adds readable status-effect information to JEI, REI, and EMI, and improves effect details in supported inventory screens.

Operation and result: Open an effect entry or inspect a potion and its uses to see what the effect does, where it comes from, and which related items provide it. Supported active-effect icons can also expose the same explanation.

Example: Before drinking an unfamiliar modded potion, check its effect page to understand the benefit, penalty, and duration context.
fml.menu.mods.info.description.jei:
Overview: Provides the main searchable browser for items, blocks, recipes, and uses from Minecraft and installed mods.

Operation and result: Type in the JEI search box, select an item, and use the configured Recipe, Uses, or Bookmark controls to inspect how it is crafted, where it is consumed, or keep it for later. Recipe-transfer controls can place available ingredients into a compatible crafting screen.

Example: Search for brass, view its recipe to find the required Create processing step, then view its uses to see every machine or component that consumes it.
fml.menu.mods.info.description.jeresourc:
Overview: Extends JEI with source information that normal crafting recipes cannot show, including world generation, mob drops, chest loot, plant drops, and supported villager trades.

Operation and result: Find an item in JEI and open its resource or usage categories to inspect where it generates, which creatures drop it, or which loot sources can contain it. JER only reports collected data; it does not change generation or loot chances.

Example: Inspect a rare ore to compare its dimension, height range, and distribution before choosing where to mine.
fml.menu.mods.info.description.jecharact:
Overview: Adds full Pinyin and Pinyin-initial matching to JEI and other supported search interfaces.

Operation and result: Enter the Pinyin spelling or the initials of a Chinese item name in the search box. Matching is added to the existing search index, so the normal English name, mod-name filters, and JEI search syntax remain available.

Example: Typing `hongshi` or `hs` can locate items whose Chinese name contains 红石 without switching the keyboard to Chinese input.
fml.menu.mods.info.description.appleskin:
Overview: Adds food and hunger information to item tooltips and the HUD so meals can be compared before eating.

Operation and result: Hover a food item to see its hunger and saturation values. While holding or eating food, the hunger bar can preview the restored points and saturation, while optional overlays expose exhaustion and estimated regeneration information.

Example: Compare an apple with a cooked meal to see which one restores more hunger now and which keeps the player full for longer.
codex_catalogue_bridge.mousetweaks.scali: Multiple Wheel Clicks Move Multiple Items
codex_catalogue_bridge.mousetweaks.direc: Down to Push, Up to Pull
Up to Push, Down to Pull
fml.menu.mods.info.description.mousetwea:
Overview: Adds faster mouse controls for moving and distributing items in inventories and containers.

Operation and result: Right-drag a held stack across compatible slots to place items repeatedly. Left-drag can rapidly collect or place matching stacks when enabled. Hover a stack and scroll to transfer items between the open container and the player inventory; by default, scrolling down pushes items into the container and scrolling up pulls them back, unless the direction setting is changed.

Example: In a chest, hover cobblestone in your inventory and scroll down to store it, then hover the chest stack and scroll up to take it back without shift-clicking each stack.
fml.menu.mods.info.description.passivese:
Overview: Stops the Creative inventory search box from automatically capturing keyboard input when the screen or a searchable tab opens.

Operation and result: Click the search field when you want to type, then click anywhere outside it to return keyboard control to normal inventory shortcuts. The field can be focused again on every later click.

Example: Opening Creative inventory no longer sends movement or hotbar keys into search, but clicking the field still allows an immediate item search.
fml.menu.mods.info.description.durabilit:
Overview: Adds readable durability information to tooltips for tools, weapons, armor, and other damageable items.

Operation and result: Hover a damageable item to see its remaining durability using the configured numeric value, condition text, or durability-bar style. Client configuration can limit the display to vanilla equipment or hide selected mod namespaces.

Example: Check a pickaxe before a long mining trip to see whether it is nearly broken instead of estimating from the small vanilla bar alone.
